Ten agents.
Ten branches.
One afternoon.

Dispatch AI coding agents in parallel, each in its own worktree.
Review the diffs, merge the wins, toss the rest.

Works with Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini · Every change isolated in its own git worktree · Free, open source, no extra platform fee

## Screenshots | Multiple agents in parallel | Focused view on a single task | | ----------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- | | ![Overview](screens/islands-overview.png) | ![Focus view](screens/islands-focus-view.png) | | **Diff review with inline comments** | **AI Arena — race agents head-to-head** | | ![Diff review](screens/diff-dialog-code-comments.png) | ![AI Arena](screens/ai-arena-mode.png) | ## Why Parallel Code? - **Use the AI coding tools you already trust** — [Claude Code](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code), [Codex CLI](https://github.com/openai/codex), [Gemini CLI](https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli), and [Copilot CLI](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/concepts/agents/about-copilot-cli) — all from one interface. - **Free and open source** — no extra subscription required. MIT licensed. - **Keep every change isolated and reviewable** — each task gets its own git branch and worktree automatically. - **Run agents in parallel, not in sequence** — five agents on five features at the same time, zero conflicts. - **See every session in one place** — switch context without losing momentum. - **Control everything keyboard-first** — every action has a shortcut, mouse optional. - **Monitor progress from your phone** — scan a QR code, watch agents work over Wi-Fi or Tailscale. - **Ask about code with any LLM** — the inline code Q&A feature supports [Claude Code](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) (default) or [MiniMax](https://www.minimax.io/) M2.7 (204K context) — configurable in Settings.
How does it compare? | Approach | What's missing | | -------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Multiple terminal windows / tmux** | No GUI, no automatic git isolation — you manage worktrees, branches, and merges by hand | | **VS Code extensions** (Kilo Code, Roo Code, etc.) | Tied to VS Code; no true parallel worktree isolation between agents | | **Running agents sequentially** | One task at a time — blocks your workflow while each agent finishes |
## How it works When you create a task, Parallel Code: 1. Creates a new git branch from your main branch 2. Sets up a [git worktree](https://git-scm.com/docs/git-worktree) so the agent works in a separate directory 3. Symlinks `node_modules` and other gitignored directories into the worktree 4. Spawns the AI agent in that worktree When you're happy with the result, merge the branch back to main from the sidebar.
More features - Tiled panel layout with drag-to-reorder - **Focus mode** — single-task layout with a clean two-column view on wide screens (`Ctrl+Shift+F`) - Built-in diff viewer with inline review comments and per-commit navigation - **Steps tracking panel** — engineering-manager-style timeline of agent progress (writes to `.claude/steps.json`) - **Notes panel per task** — jot ideas, then send the notes straight to the agent as a prompt - **PR CI status watcher** — desktop notification when GitHub checks settle - Shell terminals per task, scoped to the worktree - **Direct mode** for working on the main branch without isolation, plus support for **folders without a git repo** - **Existing worktree import** — bring already-created worktrees into Parallel Code - **Sandboxing with project-specific Dockerfiles** — drop a `.parallel-code/Dockerfile` into the project and tasks run inside it - **Coverage radar** — per-file test-coverage badges in the Changed Files panel - **Configurable keyboard shortcuts** with per-agent presets - 10 themes — Islands Dark, Minimal, Graphite, Midnight, Classic, Indigo, Ember, Glacier, Zenburnesque, Workbench - State persists across restarts - macOS and Linux
## Demo

▶ Watch the showcase (MP4)

## Getting Started 1. **Download** the latest release for your platform from the [releases page](https://github.com/johannesjo/parallel-code/releases/latest): - **macOS** — `.dmg` (universal) - **Linux** — `.AppImage` or `.deb` 2. **Install at least one AI coding CLI:** [Claude Code](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code), [Codex CLI](https://github.com/openai/codex), [Gemini CLI](https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli), [Antigravity CLI](https://antigravity.google/), or [Copilot CLI](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/concepts/agents/about-copilot-cli) 3. **Open Parallel Code**, point it at a git repo, and start dispatching tasks.
Antigravity CLI: run natively, not in Docker isolation Antigravity (`agy`) signs in interactively and caches credentials in your OS keyring (Keychain/libsecret). The keyring cannot be reached from inside a Linux container — it needs a secret-service daemon the agent container doesn't run — and `agy` has no API-key fallback, so **Docker-isolated Antigravity tasks cannot authenticate. Run Antigravity as a native (non-Docker) task.** The bundled image still ships the `agy` binary, and `~/.gemini/antigravity-cli` (settings/plugins) is shared when "Share agent auth" is enabled, so Docker support is forward-compatible if a file-based or API-key auth path appears later.
Build from source ```sh git clone https://github.com/johannesjo/parallel-code.git cd parallel-code npm install npm run dev ``` Requires [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/) v18+.
Keyboard Shortcuts `Ctrl` = `Cmd` on macOS. | Shortcut | Action | | ---------------------- | ---------------------------------- | | **Tasks** | | | `Ctrl+N` | New task | | `Ctrl+Shift+A` | New task (alternative) | | `Ctrl+Enter` | Send prompt | | `Ctrl+Shift+M` | Merge task to main | | `Ctrl+Shift+P` | Push to remote | | `Ctrl+W` | Close focused terminal session | | `Ctrl+Shift+W` | Close active task | | **Navigation** | | | `Alt+Arrows` | Focus pane/task in arrow direction | | `Alt+Shift+Left/Right` | Move task (macOS: `Ctrl+Shift`) | | `Ctrl+B` | Toggle sidebar | | `Ctrl+Shift+F` | Toggle focus mode | | **Terminals** | | | `Ctrl+Shift+T` | New shell terminal | | `Ctrl+Shift+D` | New standalone terminal | | **App** | | | `Ctrl+,` | Open settings | | `Ctrl+/` or `F1` | Show all shortcuts | | `Ctrl+0` | Reset zoom | | `Ctrl+Scroll` | Adjust zoom | | `Escape` | Close dialog |

cmux

A Ghostty-based macOS terminal with vertical tabs and notifications for AI coding agents

English | 日本語 | Tiếng Việt | 简体中文 | 繁體中文 | 한국어 | Deutsch | Español | Français | Italiano | Dansk | Polski | Русский | Bosanski | العربية | Norsk | Português (Brasil) | ไทย | Türkçe | ភាសាខ្មែរ | Українська

▶ Demo video · The Zen of cmux

## Features

Notification rings

Panes get a blue ring and tabs light up when coding agents need your attention

Notification panel

See all pending notifications in one place, jump to the most recent unread

In-app browser

Split a browser alongside your terminal with a scriptable API ported from agent-browser

Vertical + horizontal tabs

Sidebar shows git branch, linked PR status/number, working directory, listening ports, and latest notification text. Split horizontally and vertically.

SSH

cmux ssh user@remote creates a workspace for a remote machine. Browser panes route through the remote network so localhost just works. Drag an image into a remote session to upload via scp.

Claude Code Teams

cmux claude-teams runs Claude Code's teammate mode with one command. Teammates spawn as native splits with sidebar metadata and notifications. No tmux required.
- **Browser import** — Import cookies, history, and sessions from Chrome, Firefox, Arc, and 20+ browsers so browser panes start authenticated - **Custom commands** — Define project-specific actions in [`cmux.json`](https://cmux.com/docs/custom-commands) that launch from the command palette - **Programmable** — CLI and socket API to create workspaces, split panes, send keystrokes, and automate the browser - **Native macOS app** — Built with Swift and AppKit, not Electron. Fast startup, low memory. - **Ghostty compatible** — Reads your existing `~/.config/ghostty/config` for themes, fonts, and colors - **GPU-accelerated** — Powered by libghostty for smooth rendering - **Keyboard shortcuts** — [Extensive shortcuts](https://cmux.com/docs/keyboard-shortcuts) for workspaces, splits, browser, and more - **Open source** — Free and GPL-licensed ## Install ### DMG (recommended) Open the `.dmg` and drag cmux to your Applications folder. cmux auto-updates via Sparkle, so you only need to download once. ### Homebrew ```bash brew tap manaflow-ai/cmux brew install --cask cmux ``` To update later: ```bash brew upgrade --cask cmux ``` On first launch, macOS may ask you to confirm opening an app from an identified developer. Click **Open** to proceed. ## Why cmux? I run a lot of Claude Code and Codex sessions in parallel. I was using Ghostty with a bunch of split panes, and relying on native macOS notifications to know when an agent needed me. But Claude Code's notification body is always just "Claude is waiting for your input" with no context, and with enough tabs open I couldn't even read the titles anymore. I tried a few coding orchestrators but most of them were Electron/Tauri apps and the performance bugged me. I also just prefer the terminal since GUI orchestrators lock you into their workflow. So I built cmux as a native macOS app in Swift/AppKit. It uses libghostty for terminal rendering and reads your existing Ghostty config for themes, fonts, and colors. The main additions are the sidebar and notification system. The sidebar has vertical tabs that show git branch, linked PR status/number, working directory, listening ports, and the latest notification text for each workspace. The notification system picks up terminal sequences (OSC 9/99/777) and has a CLI (`cmux notify`) you can wire into agent hooks for Claude Code, OpenCode, etc. When an agent is waiting, its pane gets a blue ring and the tab lights up in the sidebar, so I can tell which one needs me across splits and tabs. Cmd+Shift+U jumps to the most recent unread. The in-app browser has a scriptable API ported from [agent-browser](https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser). Agents can snapshot the accessibility tree, get element refs, click, fill forms, and evaluate JS. You can split a browser pane next to your terminal and have Claude Code interact with your dev server directly. Everything is scriptable through the CLI and socket API — create workspaces/tabs, split panes, send keystrokes, open URLs in the browser. ## The Zen of cmux cmux is not prescriptive about how developers hold their tools. It's a terminal and browser with a CLI, and the rest is up to you. cmux is a primitive, not a solution. It gives you a terminal, a browser, notifications, workspaces, splits, tabs, and a CLI to control all of it. cmux doesn't force you into an opinionated way to use coding agents. What you build with the primitives is yours. The best developers have always built their own tools. Nobody has figured out the best way to work with agents yet, and the teams building closed products definitely haven't either. The developers closest to their own codebases will figure it out first. Give a million developers composable primitives and they'll collectively find the most efficient workflows faster than any product team could design top-down. ## Documentation For more info on how to configure cmux, [head over to our docs](https://cmux.com/docs/getting-started?utm_source=readme). ## Keyboard Shortcuts ### Workspaces | Shortcut | Action | |----------|--------| | ⌘ N | New workspace | | ⌘ 1–8 | Jump to workspace 1–8 | | ⌘ 9 | Jump to last workspace | | ⌃ ⌘ ] | Next workspace | | ⌃ ⌘ [ | Previous workspace | | ⌘ ⇧ W | Close workspace | | ⌘ ⇧ R | Rename workspace | | ⌥ ⌘ E | Edit workspace description | | ⌘ B | Toggle sidebar | | ⌥ ⌘ B | Toggle right sidebar | | ⌘ ⇧ E | Toggle right sidebar focus | ### Surfaces | Shortcut | Action | |----------|--------| | ⌘ T | New surface | | ⌘ ⇧ ] | Next surface | | ⌘ ⇧ [ | Previous surface | | ⌃ Tab | Next surface | | ⌃ ⇧ Tab | Previous surface | | ⌃ 1–8 | Jump to surface 1–8 | | ⌃ 9 | Jump to last surface | [Showing lines 1-300 of 568. [Download](https://emdash.sh/download) · [Docs](https://emdash.sh/docs) · [Releases](https://github.com/generalaction/emdash/releases/latest) · [Discord](https://discord.gg/f2fv7YxuR2) · [Contributing](CONTRIBUTING.md)
[![Apache 2.0 License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache_2.0-555555.svg?labelColor=333333&color=666666)](./LICENSE.md) [![Downloads](https://img.shields.io/github/downloads/generalaction/emdash/total?labelColor=333333&color=666666)](https://github.com/generalaction/emdash/releases) [![GitHub Stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/generalaction/emdash?labelColor=333333&color=666666&logo=github)](https://github.com/generalaction/emdash) [![Last Commit](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/generalaction/emdash?labelColor=333333&color=666666)](https://github.com/generalaction/emdash/commits/main) [![Commit Activity](https://img.shields.io/github/commit-activity/m/generalaction/emdash?labelColor=333333&color=666666)](https://github.com/generalaction/emdash/graphs/commit-activity) [![Discord](https://img.shields.io/badge/Discord-join-%235462eb?labelColor=%235462eb&logo=discord&logoColor=%23f5f5f5)](https://discord.gg/f2fv7YxuR2) [![Follow @emdashsh on X](https://img.shields.io/twitter/follow/emdashsh?logo=X&color=%23f5f5f5)](https://twitter.com/intent/follow?screen_name=emdashsh)
Emdash is a desktop app for running AI coding agents in parallel. Each task runs in its own Git worktree, so you can explore multiple fixes or features at once, review the diffs, and merge what works. It works with local projects and remote machines over SSH. Bring the CLI agents you already use: Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Amp, and more. ## What You Can Do - Run multiple coding agents at once without juggling terminals. - Keep every agent isolated in its own Git worktree and branch. - Send issues and tickets from Linear, GitHub, Jira, GitLab, Asana, Featurebase, Monday.com, Forgejo, or Plain into an agent. - Review diffs, create pull requests, inspect CI checks, and merge from one place. - Work locally or on your own remote machines over SSH/SFTP. ## Installation | Platform | Install | | --- | --- | | macOS | `brew install --cask emdash` · [Apple Silicon](https://github.com/generalaction/emdash/releases/latest/download/emdash-arm64.dmg) · [Intel](https://github.com/generalaction/emdash/releases/latest/download/emdash-x64.dmg) | | Windows | [Installer](https://github.com/generalaction/emdash/releases/latest/download/emdash-x64.msi) · [Portable](https://github.com/generalaction/emdash/releases/latest/download/emdash-x64.exe) | | Linux | [AppImage](https://github.com/generalaction/emdash/releases/latest/download/emdash-x86_64.AppImage) · [Debian package](https://github.com/generalaction/emdash/releases/latest/download/emdash-amd64.deb) | See the [latest release](https://github.com/generalaction/emdash/releases/latest) for all desktop builds. ## Agents Emdash detects installed provider CLIs automatically. It supports agents like Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, Amp, Devin, Qwen Code, Droid, and GitHub Copilot. See [Providers](https://emdash.sh/docs/providers) for the full list, setup commands, and provider-specific behavior. ## Remote Projects Connect to remote machines with SSH/SFTP and run the same parallel workflow on remote codebases. Emdash supports SSH agent, key, and password authentication, with credentials stored in your OS keychain. See [Remote Projects](https://emdash.sh/docs/remote-projects) and [Bring Your Own Infrastructure](https://emdash.sh/docs/bring-your-own-infrastructure) for setup details. ## Privacy Emdash is local-first. App state is stored in a local SQLite database, and Emdash does not send your code or chats to Emdash servers. Agent CLIs may send code, prompts, and context to their own providers. Their data handling depends on the provider you choose. Telemetry is optional and can be disabled in Settings or by launching with: ```bash TELEMETRY_ENABLED=false ``` See [Telemetry](https://emdash.sh/docs/telemetry) for details. ## Contributing Contributions are welcome. Read the [Contributing Guide](CONTRIBUTING.md), open an issue, or join the [Discord](https://discord.gg/f2fv7YxuR2). ## License Licensed under the [Apache-2.0 license](LICENSE.md).

데이터가 충분히 모였습니다. 종합 분석을 작성하겠습니다.

---

# Agent-Native IDE 종합 비교 분석 (2026년 7월 기준)

## 1. 배경: Agent-Native IDE란?

2026년에 들어서며 "에이전트를 위해 태어난 IDE"라는 카테고리가 명확히 형성되었습니다. 기존 IDE(Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code)가 **AI 기능을 IDE에 붙이는** 접근이라면, Agent-Native IDE는 **여러 CLI 코딩 에이전트(Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI 등)를 병렬로 실행·관리·리뷰하기 위해 설계된** 환경입니다. 핵심 차별점은:

- **Git Worktree 기반 격리** — 각 에이전트마다 독립된 브랜치/디렉토리
- **멀티 에이전트 동시 실행** — 한 화면에서 5~20개 에이전트 모니터링
- **Diff 리뷰 + 머지 워크플로우** — 에이전트가 만든 변경사항을 한 곳에서 검토
- **BYOA (Bring Your Own Agent)** — 자기 구독으로 어떤 CLI 에이전트든 실행

---

## 2. 조사 대상 도구 (10종)

| # | 도구 | GitHub Stars | 언어 | 라이선스 | 플랫폼 | 제작사 |
|---|------|-------------|------|----------|--------|--------|
| 1 | **Orca** | 18,327 | TypeScript | MIT | macOS·Win·Linux + 모바일 | stablyai |
| 2 | **cmux** | 24,412 | Swift | GPL | macOS | manaflow-ai |
| 3 | **Superset** | 12,399 | TypeScript | Other | macOS | superset-sh (YC) |
| 4 | **Emdash** | 5,149 | TypeScript | Apache-2.0 | macOS·Win·Linux | generalaction (YC W26) |
| 5 | **Claude Squad** | 8,108 | Go | AGPL-3.0 | macOS·Linux | smtg-ai |
| 6 | **Parallel Code** | 838 | TypeScript | MIT | macOS·Linux | johannesjo |
| 7 | **Conductor** | 폐쇄 | — | 폐쇄 | macOS | Melty Labs |
| 8 | **Nimbalyst** | (Crystal 후속) | — | 미공개 | 크로스플랫폼 + iOS | Stravu |
| 9 | **Vibe Kanban** | (커뮤니티) | TypeScript | Apache-2.0 | Web | BloopAI (서비스 종료) |
| 10 | **Intent** | 폐쇄 | — | 폐쇄 | macOS | Augment Code |

> **참고 — 더 넓은 생태계:** 전통 IDE도 agent-native 기능을 흡수하고 있습니다. **Cursor 3**(2026.04 발표, Agents Window + `/worktree` `/best-of-n`), **Windsurf Wave 13**(병렬 Cascade 세션), **Google Antigravity**(멀티 에이전트 + 무료) 등. 하지만 이들은 "IDE에 에이전트를 붙인" 접근으로, 본 분석에서는 BYOA 원칙으로 어떤 CLI 에이전트든 실행하는 순수 Agent-Native 도구에 집중합니다.

---

## 3. 다차원 평가 (10점 만점)

| 도구 | 기술력 (20%) | 안정성 (15%) | 편의성 (15%) | 생태계 (15%) | 확장성 (15%) | 플랫폼 (10%) | 오픈소스 (10%) | **종합** |
|------|:-----------:|:-----------:|:-----------:|:-----------:|:-----------:|:-----------:|:------------:|:-------:|
| **🥇 Orca** | 9 | 7 | 9 | 8 | 9 | 10 | 8 | **8.6** |
| **🥈 Emdash** | 8 | 7 | 8 | 9 | 8 | 10 | 9 | **8.3** |
| **🥉 Superset** | 8 | 8 | 8 | 9 | 8 | 3 | 6 | **7.5** |
| **cmux** | 8 | 6 | 8 | 8 | 7 | 3 | 7 | **7.0** |
| **Parallel Code** | 7 | 7 | 7 | 6 | 6 | 6 | 8 | **6.7** |
| **Claude Squad** | 6 | 9 | 6 | 7 | 5 | 6 | 6 | **6.5** |
| **Vibe Kanban** | 7 | 4 | 7 | 7 | 5 | 8 | 6 | **6.3** |
| **Nimbalyst** | 7 | 5 | 7 | 5 | 6 | 8 | 5 | **6.2** |
| **Intent** | 9 | 5 | 7 | 6 | 7 | 3 | 2 | **6.1** |
| **Conductor** | 7 | 7 | 8 | 5 | 5 | 3 | 2 | **5.7** |

### 평가 기준 정의

| 지표 | 핵심 평가 요소 |
|------|---------------|
| **기술력** | 격리 모델(worktree/container), 조정 깊이(세션 매니저↔스펙 기반), 아키텍처 품질, 고유 기술(Design Mode, Computer Use, 포트 주입 등) |
| **안정성** | 이슈/스타 비율, 릴리스 주기, 코드베이스 성숙도, 런타임 안정성(native vs Electron) |
| **편의성** | 설치 용이성, 시각적 diff 리뷰, 키보드 단축키, 알림 시스템, 워크플로우 폴리싱 |
| **생태계** | 지원 에이전트 수, 티켓 통합(Linear/GitHub/Jira), CLI/SDK/MCP 제공, 커뮤니티 규모 |
| **확장성** | 원격 실행(SSH), 모바일 동반 앱, 병렬 에이전트 용량, 프로그래밍 가능성 |
| **플랫폼** | OS 지원 범위 (macOS / Windows / Linux / 모바일) |
| **오픈소스** | 라이선스 자유도, 벤더 독립성, 커뮤니티 주도도 |

---

## 4. 도구별 상세 분석

### 🥇 1위 — Orca (stablyai/orca) · 종합 8.6

```
GitHub: 18,327★ · 1,448 forks · 1,361 issues (7.4%) · MIT · TypeScript/Electron
플랫폼: macOS · Windows · Linux · iOS · Android
```

**장점:**
- **유일한 풀 크로스플랫폼 + 모바일** — iOS/Android 동반 앱으로 에이전트 완료 알림 수신 및 후속 지시 가능
- **Design Mode** — 실제 Chromium 창에서 UI 요소 클릭 시 HTML/CSS/스크린샷이 에이전트 프롬프트로 직송. 다른 도구에 없는 독창적 기능
- **SSH Worktrees** — 원격 머신에서 에이전트 실행, 자동 재연결 + 포트 포워딩
- **Computer Use** — 에이전트가 데스크톱 앱과 실제 UI 조작 가능
- **GitHub & Linear 네이티브** — PR/이슈/보드를 앱 내에서 브라우징, 워크트리에서 바로 열기
- **Diff 주석(Annotate)** — diff 라인에 코멘트를 달아 에이전트에게 피드백 전송
- **일일 릴리스** — 매일 기능 출시, changelog가 사실상 기능 목록
- Ghostty-class 터미널 (WebGL 렌더링), 무한 분할, 재시작 후에도 스크롤백 보존

**단점:**
- Electron 기반 — cmux(native Swift) 대비 메모리/시작 속도 열세
- 1,361개 미해결 이슈 — 활발함의 반증이지만 관리 부담 존재
- MIT이지만 stablyai가 단일 주도 — 벤더 의존도

**한줄평:** *기능·플랫폼·확장성 3박자 최강의 올라운더. 모바일 모니터링이 필요한 분산 팀에 첫 번째 선택지.*

---

### 🥈 2위 — Emdash (generalaction/emdash) · 종합 8.3

```
GitHub: 5,149★ · 534 forks · 132 issues (2.6%) · Apache-2.0 · TypeScript/Electron
플랫폼: macOS · Windows · Linux · YC W26
```

**장점:**
- **가장 넓은 티켓 통합** — Linear, GitHub, Jira, GitLab, Asana, Monday.com, Forgejo, Plain (8개 플랫폼)
- **$EMDASH_PORT 주입** — 포트 충돌 문제를 설정 레벨에서 해결. 다른 도구가 놓치는 "공유 런타임" 문제를 직접 해공
- **가장 넓은 에이전트 지원** — Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, Amp, Devin, Qwen Code, Droid, Copilot 등 28+ 자동 감지
- **SSH/SFTP 원격 프로젝트** — 원격 코드베이스에서 동일한 병렬 워크플로우, OS 키체인 인증
- **Local-first + 프라이버시** — SQLite 로컬 저장, 코드/채팅을 서버로 전송하지 않음
- **크로스플랫폼 + Apache-2.0** — 가장 관대한 라이선스 + 3 OS 지원
- 이슈/스타 비율 2.6% — 안정성 지표 우수

**단점:**
- 모바일 동반 앱 없음
- 에이전트 간 조정(협업) 불가 — 각자 독립 실행만
- 2026년 초 출시로 커뮤니티가 아직 성장 단계

**한줄평:** *엔터프라이즈 팀을 위한 최적의 선택. 포트 주입·티켓 통합·프라이버시 3박자로 실전에서 가장 실용적.*

---

### 🥉 3위 — Superset (superset-sh/superset) · 종합 7.5

```
GitHub: 12,399★ · 1,076 forks · 323 issues (2.6%) · Other · TypeScript/Electron
플랫폼: macOS only · YC-backed
```

**장점:**
- **가장 다양한 접근면(Surface)** — 데스크톱 앱 + CLI + TypeScript SDK + MCP 서버. 어디서든 워크스페이스 제어
- **가장 넓은 에이전트 지원** — Amp, Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Droid, Gemini, Copilot, Mastra, Mistral Vibe, OpenCode, Pi, Polygraph + 커스텀 에이전트
- **자동화(Automations)** — 스케줄 기반 에이전트 세션 실행
- **Slack & Linear 통합** — Slack 메시지/Linear 이슈에서 워크스페이스 생성
- **인앱 브라우저 + 포트 감지** — 워크스페이스별 dev 서버 미리보기
- 커스텀 에이전트 추가(아이콘 포함), 워크스페이스 프리셋
- 이슈/스타 비율 2.6% — 안정성 양호

**단점:**
- **macOS 전용** — Windows/Linux 빌드 없음. 가장 치명적인 약점
- Bun 런타임, Caddy 등 외부 의존성 필요
- "Other" 라이선스 — 오픈소스 자유도 불투명
- iOS 앱 "출시 예정" 단계

**한줄평:** *기능 깊이와 SDK/MCP 생태계는 최상급이지만, macOS 전용이 발목. 프로그래밍 가능성을 중시하는 macOS 개발자에게 최적.*

---

### 4위 — cmux (manaflow-ai/cmux) · 종합 7.0

```
GitHub: 24,412★ · 1,970 forks · 3,446 issues (14.1%) · GPL · Swift (native)
플랫폼: macOS only
```

**장점:**
- **GitHub 스타 수 최다 (24.4K)** — 커뮤니티 규모 최대
- **네이티브 성능** — Swift/AppKit + libghostty GPU 렌더링. Electron 대비 압도적 시작 속도·메모리
- **철학이 명확** — "원시 도구(primitive)지 솔루션이 아니다". 워크플로우를 강제하지 않음
- **알림 시스템** — OSC 9/99/777 시퀀스 감지, 파란 링 + 탭 점등, `cmux notify` CLI
- **인앱 브라우저** — agent-browser API 포팅, 접근성 트리 스냅샷·클릭·폼 입력·JS 실행
- **SSH** — `cmux ssh user@remote`로 원격 워크스페이스, 브라우저가 원격 네트워크 경유
- **Claude Code Teams** — `cmux claude-teams` 한 명령으로 teammate 모드, tmux 불필요
- Ghostty config 호환 — 기존 테마/폰트/색상 그대로 사용
- CLI + socket API로 전체 자동화

**단점:**
- **macOS 전용** — 플랫폼 점수 최하위
- **터미널이지 IDE가 아님** — worktree 관리, diff 뷰어, PR 생성 기능 없음. 다른 도구로 보완 필요
- **이슈 3,446개 (14.1%)** — 비율 최고. 복잡한 네이티브 앱의 관리 부담
- 모바일 없음

**한줄평:** *성능과 철학은 최강이지만 "터미널" 한계. worktree/diff 리뷰가 필요하면 Orca/Emdash/Superset과 조합해야 함.*

---

### 5위 — Parallel Code (johannesjo/parallel-code) · 종합 6.7

```
GitHub: 838★ · 111 forks · 39 issues (4.7%) · MIT · TypeScript/Electron + SolidJS
플랫폼: macOS · Linux
```

**장점:**
- **Docker 샌드박싱** — 프로젝트별 `.parallel-code/Dockerfile`로 완전 격리. 다른 OSS 도구에 없는 기능
- **AI Arena** — 에이전트 간 헤드투헤드 경쟁, 승자 머지
- **커버리지 레이더** — 변경 파일별 테스트 커버리지 배지
- **Steps 추적 패널** — `.claude/steps.json` 기반 에이전트 진행 타임라인
- 모바일 모니터링 (QR 코드 → Wi-Fi/Tailscale)
- 10개 테마, 키보드 퍼스트, 포커스 모드
- MIT 라이선스, 완전 무료

**단점:**
- 커뮤니티 최소 (838★) — 장기 유지보수 불확실
- Windows 미지원
- SSH/원격 워크트리 없음
- 단일 개발자(johannesjo) 프로젝트

**한줄평:** *Docker 샌드박싱과 AI Arena는 독창적이지만, 커뮤니티가 너무 작아 프로덕션 신뢰성이 부족.*

---

### 6위 — Claude Squad (smtg-ai/claude-squad) · 종합 6.5

```
GitHub: 8,108★ · 585 forks · 52 issues (0.6%) · AGPL-3.0 · Go (TUI)
플랫폼: macOS · Linux (tmux 필요)
```

**장점:**
- **압도적 안정성** — 이슈/스타 비율 0.6%, Go 바이너리, 단순 아키텍처(tmux + worktree)
- **가장 빠른 설정** — `brew install claude-squad` → 5초 내 6개 세션 실행
- 키보드 퍼스트 TUI — vim 스타일 네비게이션, 학습 곡선 완만
- Claude Code, Codex, Aider, Gemini, OpenCode, Amp 지원
- `--autoyes` 플래그로 무인 자동 수락 (실험적)

**단점:**
- **시각적 diff 리뷰 약함** — 별도 도구 필요
- **원격/SSH/모바일 전무** — 로컬 단일 머신만
- AGPL-3.0 — 상업적 임베딩 시 법무 검토 필요
- Windows 미지원 (tmux 의존)

**한줄평:** *터미널 파워 유저의 최단 경로. 안정성은 최고지만 시각 리뷰와 확장성이 없어 병목.*

---

### 7위 — Vibe Kanban (BloopAI/vibe-kanban) · 종합 6.3

```
Apache-2.0 · TypeScript · Web app · Bloop 서비스 종료 → 커뮤니티 유지보수
```

**장점:**
- 칸반 보드 = worktree = 에이전트. 직관적 메타포
- MCP 서버로 카드 자동 분해 (자율적 태스크 분해에 가장 근접)
- 10+ 에이전트 지원, 인앱 브라우저(디바이스 에뮬레이션 포함)
- 셀프 호스팅 가능, 팀 친화적

**단점:**
- **Bloop 서비스 종료** — 유료 구독 환불, 클라우드 기능 중단. 커뮤니티 유지보수로 전환
- 하드 태스크 의존성(B가 A를 기다림) 미지원
- 워크트리 20개 시 디스크 부담
- 미래 유지보수 속도가 커뮤니티 기여에 전적 의존

**한줄평:** *칸반 메타포와 MCP 분해는 뛰어나지만, Bloop 종료로 미래가 불확실. 도박성 투자.*

---

### 8위 — Nimbalyst (Crystal 후속) · 종합 6.2

```
크로스플랫폼 데스크톱 + iOS · Stravu · 라이선스 미공개
```

**장점:**
- worktree가 세션 객체의 일급 시민 — 시작 시 원클릭 옵션
- 인라인 diff, 시각적 파일 사이드바, GUI 리뷰-머지
- 크로스플랫폼 + iOS 동반 앱
- 마크다운/Excalidraw/목업 시각 편집 내장

**단점:**
- 2026년 2월 Crystal에서 리브랜딩 — 가장 어린 프로젝트
- 커뮤니티·문서·평판 부족
- 벤더 단일 주도, 라이선스 불투명
- 에이전트 지원 좁음 (Claude Code, Codex)

**한줄평:** *잘설계됐지만 너무 새롭고 신뢰 데이터가 부족. 6개월 후 재평가 권장.*

---

### 9위 — Intent (Augment Code) · 종합 6.1

```
macOS only · 공개 베타 · 폐쇄 소스 · Augment 크레딧 기반
```

**장점:**
- **스펙 기반 조정(Spec-Driven)** — Living spec이 모든 에이전트의 단일 진실 원천. 스펙을 한 번 수정하면 모든 활성 에이전트에 전파
- **Coordinator → Implementor → Verifier** 3단계 에이전트 분업
- 크로스 서비스 리팩토링에서 진가 — 필드명 변경 등 스펙 레벨 전파
- BYOA (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode + Auggie)
- Context Engine을 MCP 서버로 노출

**단점:**
- **폐쇄 소스** — 오픈소스 점수 최하위
- **macOS only** (Windows 대기자 명단, Linux 미발표)
- Augment 크레딧 사용 — 기존 Claude/Cursor 구독과 비용 중복
- 공개 베타 — 프로덕션 안정성 미검증

**한줄평:** *스펙 기반 조정 개념은 혁신적이지만, 폐쇄·macOS·베타 3중 제약. 크로스 서비스 팀에만 가치.*

---

### 10위 — Conductor (Melty Labs) · 종합 5.7

```
macOS only · 무료 · 폐쇄 소스 · conductor.build
```

**장점:**
- macOS에서 가장 세련된 대시보드 — diff-first 리뷰, 깔끔한 폴리싱
- 무료 + BYO Claude/Codex/Cursor/OpenCode 로그인
- 기존 VS Code/JetBrains 옆에 두고 쓰는 오케스트레이터로 최적

**단점:**
- **폐쇄 소스** — 코드 감사 불가, 벤더 종속
- **macOS only** — 크로스플랫폼 "점진적 추가 중"
- CLI/SDK/MCP 없음 — 프로그래밍 불가
- 원격/SSH/모바일 전무
- 에이전트 지원 좁음 (4종)

**한줄평:** *Mac 사용자의 "가장 쉬운 시작점"이지만, 폐쇄·단일 플랫폼·확장성 부재로 장기 신뢰성 최하위.*

---

## 5. 종합 순위 요약

| 순위 | 도구 | 종합 | 핵심 강점 | 핵심 약점 |
|:----:|------|:----:|-----------|-----------|
| 1 | **Orca** | 8.6 | 크로스플랫폼+모바일, Design Mode, 일일 릴리스 | Electron, 이슈 다수 |
| 2 | **Emdash** | 8.3 | 포트 주입, 8개 티켓 통합, 28+ 에이전트, 프라이버시 | 모바일 없음, 신생 |
| 3 | **Superset** | 7.5 | CLI+SDK+MCP 3면 제공, 자동화, 최다 에이전트 | macOS 전용 |
| 4 | **cmux** | 7.0 | 네이티브 성능 최강, 최대 커뮤니티, 철학 | macOS 전용, IDE 아님(터미널) |
| 5 | **Parallel Code** | 6.7 | Docker 샌드박싱, AI Arena | 커뮤니티 최소 |
| 6 | **Claude Squad** | 6.5 | 안정성 최고(0.6%), 최단 설정 | 시각 리뷰/확장성 전무 |
| 7 | **Vibe Kanban** | 6.3 | 칸반+MCP 자동 분해 | Bloop 종료, 미래 불확실 |
| 8 | **Nimbalyst** | 6.2 | worktree 일급 시민, iOS | 너무 신생, 신뢰 데이터 부족 |
| 9 | **Intent** | 6.1 | 스펙 기반 조정 혁신 | 폐쇄·macOS·베타 |
| 10 | **Conductor** | 5.7 | Mac 최고의 폴리싱 | 폐쇄·macOS·확장성 제로 |

---

## 6. 사용 사례별 추천

| 상황 | 추천 | 이유 |
|------|------|------|
| **크로스플랫폼 팀, 모바일 모니터링 필요** | **Orca** | 유일하게 3 OS + iOS/Android 지원 |
| **엔터프라이즈, 다수 티켓 시스템 통합** | **Emdash** | 8개 티켓 플랫폼 + 포트 주입 + 프라이버시 |
| **macOS + 프로그래밍 가능한 자동화** | **Superset** | CLI + SDK + MCP 3면 제공, Automations |
| **macOS + 네이티브 성능 + 터미널 파워 유저** | **cmux** | Swift/AppKit, GPU 렌더링, 알림 시스템 |
| **터미널만으로 빠르게 시작** | **Claude Squad** | 5초 설치, 가장 안정적 |
| **Docker 격리 + 에이전트 경쟁 실험** | **Parallel Code** | AI Arena, 프로젝트별 Dockerfile |
| **스펙 기반 크로스 서비스 리팩토링** | **Intent** | Living spec 전파, Coordinator/Verifier |
| **예산 없이 무료 Mac 시작** | **Conductor** | 무료 + BYO 로그인, 가장 쉬운 진입 |

> **2026년 7월 시장 관찰:** Orca와 Emdash가 크로스플랫폼 + 오픈소스 + 풍부한 기능으로 1티어를 형성하고 있습니다. cmux는 커뮤니티 규모와 성능으로 독보적이지만, macOS 전용 + 터미널 한계로 "IDE" 카테고리에서는 4위에 머뭅니다. Superset은 기능 깊이는 최상급이지만 macOS 전용이 치명적입니다. 폐쇄 소스 도구(Conductor, Intent)는 오픈소스 자유도에서 근본적 불리를 안고 있습니다.

arguments.content:
# 🚀 Awesome Agent-Native IDE ### A curated, ranked comparison of IDEs and orchestrators built for the AI coding agent era. [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/pinion05/awesome-agent-ide?style=for-the-badge&logo=github)](https://github.com/pinion05/awesome-agent-ide) [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/CC0-1.0-blue?style=for-the-badge)](LICENSE) [![Last Updated](https://img.shields.io/badge/Updated-2026--07-lightgrey?style=for-the-badge)](#) [![Website](https://img.shields.io/badge/🌐%20Website-GitHub%20Pages-success?style=for-the-badge)](https://pinion05.github.io/awesome-agent-ide/)
--- ## 📖 What is an Agent-Native IDE? Traditional IDEs (Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code) bolt AI features onto an existing editor. **Agent-Native IDEs** are built from the ground up to run, manage, and review **multiple CLI coding agents** (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) in parallel. The defining characteristics: | Feature | Description | |---------|-------------| | **Git Worktree Isolation** | Each agent gets its own branch and working directory, sharing one `.git` database | | **Multi-Agent Parallel Execution** | Run 5–20+ agents simultaneously, monitored from one screen | | **Diff Review + Merge Workflow** | Review agent-generated changes in one place before merging | | **BYOA (Bring Your Own Agent)** | Run any CLI agent with your own subscription — no vendor lock-in | > **Not covered here:** Traditional IDEs with AI bolted on (Cursor, Windsurf, Copilot). Those are excellent tools, but this list focuses on tools whose *primary purpose* is agent orchestration. --- ## 🏆 Ranking Overview | Rank | Tool | Score | Platform | License | Stars | One-Liner | |:----:|------|:-----:|----------|---------|:-----:|-----------| | 🥇 | [**Orca**](https://github.com/stablyai/orca) | **8.6** | macOS·Win·Linux·📱 | MIT | 18.3K | Full cross-platform + mobile, Design Mode, daily releases | | 🥈 | [**Emdash**](https://github.com/generalaction/emdash) | **8.3** | macOS·Win·Linux | Apache-2.0 | 5.1K | Port injection, 8 ticket integrations, 28+ agents | | 🥉 | [**Superset**](https://github.com/superset-sh/superset) | **7.5** | macOS | Other | 12.4K | CLI + SDK + MCP, automations, broadest agent support | | 4 | [**cmux**](https://github.com/manaflow-ai/cmux) | **7.0** | macOS | GPL | 24.4K | Native Swift performance, largest community, terminal-first | | 5 | [**Parallel Code**](https://github.com/johannesjo/parallel-code) | **6.7** | macOS·Linux | MIT | 0.8K | Docker sandboxing, AI Arena, coverage radar | | 6 | [**Claude Squad**](https://github.com/smtg-ai/claude-squad) | **6.5** | macOS·Linux | AGPL-3.0 | 8.1K | Best stability (0.6% issue ratio), fastest setup | | 7 | [**Vibe Kanban**](https://github.com/BloopAI/vibe-kanban) | **6.3** | Web | Apache-2.0 | — | Kanban + MCP auto-decomposition (community-maintained) | | 8 | [**Nimbalyst**](https://nimbalyst.com/) | **6.2** | Cross-platform·📱 | — | — | Worktree-first sessions, visual editing, iOS companion | | 9 | [**Intent**](https://www.intentapp.dev/) | **6.1** | macOS | Closed | — | Spec-driven coordination, Coordinator/Verifier agents | | 10 | [**Conductor**](https://www.conductor.build/) | **5.7** | macOS | Closed | — | Best Mac polish, free, BYO login | --- ## 📊 Scoring Methodology Each tool is scored on **7 dimensions** (1–10 scale), weighted to produce a composite score. | Dimension | Weight | What We Evaluate | |-----------|:------:|------------------| | **Technical Depth** | 20% | Isolation model (worktree/container), coordination depth, architecture quality, unique tech (Design Mode, Computer Use, port injection) | | **Stability** | 15% | Issue-to-star ratio, release cadence, codebase maturity, runtime stability (native vs Electron) | | **UX / Convenience** | 15% | Setup ease, visual diff review, keyboard shortcuts, notification system, workflow polish | | **Ecosystem** | 15% | Supported agents, ticket integrations (Linear/GitHub/Jira), CLI/SDK/MCP availability, community size | | **Scalability** | 15% | Remote execution (SSH), mobile companion, parallel capacity, programmability | | **Platform Support** | 10% | OS coverage (macOS / Windows / Linux / mobile) | | **Open Source Health** | 10% | License freedom, vendor independence, community-driven development | ### Detailed Scores | Tool | Technical (20%) | Stability (15%) | UX (15%) | Ecosystem (15%) | Scalability (15%) | Platform (10%) | OSS (10%) | **Total** | |------|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:| | **Orca** | 9 | 7 | 9 | 8 | 9 | 10 | 8 | **8.6** | | **Emdash** | 8 | 7 | 8 | 9 | 8 | 10 | 9 | **8.3** | | **Superset** | 8 | 8 | 8 | 9 | 8 | 3 | 6 | **7.5** | | **cmux** | 8 | 6 | 8 | 8 | 7 | 3 | 7 | **7.0** | | **Parallel Code** | 7 | 7 | 7 | 6 | 6 | 6 | 8 | **6.7** | | **Claude Squad** | 6 | 9 | 6 | 7 | 5 | 6 | 6 | **6.5** | | **Vibe Kanban** | 7 | 4 | 7 | 7 | 5 | 8 | 6 | **6.3** | | **Nimbalyst** | 7 | 5 | 7 | 5 | 6 | 8 | 5 | **6.2** | | **Intent** | 9 | 5 | 7 | 6 | 7 | 3 | 2 | **6.1** | | **Conductor** | 7 | 7 | 8 | 5 | 5 | 3 | 2 | **5.7** | --- ## 🔍 Detailed Analysis ### 🥇 1. Orca — stablyai/orca | | | |---|---| | **GitHub** | [stablyai/orca](https://github.com/stablyai/orca) | | **Stars / Forks / Issues** | 18,327 / 1,448 / 1,361 (7.4%) | | **Language** | TypeScript (Electron) | | **License** | MIT | | **Platform** | macOS · Windows · Linux · iOS · Android | | **Website** | [onorca.dev](https://www.onorca.dev/) | **✅ Strengths** - **Only full cross-platform + mobile** — iOS/Android companion app for monitoring and follow-ups from anywhere - **Design Mode** — Click any UI element in a real Chromium window; HTML/CSS/screenshot sent straight to the agent's prompt. Unique to Orca. - **SSH Worktrees** — Run agents on a beefy remote box with auto-reconnect and port forwarding - **Computer Use** — Agents can operate desktop apps and visible UI - **GitHub & Linear native** — Browse PRs, issues, and boards in-app; open a worktree from any task - **Annotate AI Diffs** — Drop comments on any diff line, ship them back to the agent - **Daily releases** — Ships every day; the changelog is the real feature list - Ghostty-class terminals (WebGL), infinite splits, scrollback that survives restarts **❌ Weaknesses** - Electron-based — heavier than native alternatives (cmux) - 1,361 open issues — active but management burden is real - MIT but stablyai-led — single vendor dependency > **Best for:** Distributed teams needing mobile monitoring, cross-platform support, and the richest agent-native feature set. --- ### 🥈 2. Emdash — generalaction/emdash | | | |---|---| | **GitHub** | [generalaction/emdash](https://github.com/generalaction/emdash) | | **Stars / Forks / Issues** | 5,149 / 534 / 132 (2.6%) | | **Language** | TypeScript (Electron) | | **License** | Apache-2.0 | | **Platform** | macOS · Windows · Linux | | **Website** | [emdash.sh](https://emdash.sh/) | | **Backing** | YC W26 | **✅ Strengths** - **Broadest ticket integration** — Linear, GitHub, Jira, GitLab, Asana, Monday.com, Forgejo, Plain (8 platforms) - **`$EMDASH_PORT` injection** — Solves the port-collision problem at the config level, which almost no other tool handles - **Broadest agent support** — 28+ CLI providers auto-detected (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, Amp, Devin, Qwen Code, Droid, Copilot, etc.) - **SSH/SFTP remote projects** — Same parallel workflow on remote codebases, OS keychain auth - **Local-first + privacy** — SQLite local storage, no code/chat sent to Emdash servers - Cross-platform + Apache-2.0 — most permissive license combination - Issue/star ratio 2.6% — excellent stability indicator **❌ Weaknesses** - No mobile companion app - No agent-to-agent coordination — each runs independently - Launched early 2026; community still growing > **Best for:** Enterprise teams needing ticket integration, port isolation, and cross-platform support with strong privacy guarantees. --- ### 🥉 3. Superset — superset-sh/superset | | | |---|---| | **GitHub** | [superset-sh/superset](https://github.com/superset-sh/superset) | | **Stars / Forks / Issues** | 12,399 / 1,076 / 323 (2.6%) | | **Language** | TypeScript (Electron) | | **License** | Other | | **Platform** | macOS | | **Website** | [superset.sh](https://superset.sh/) | | **Backing** | YC | **✅ Strengths** - **Most access surfaces** — Desktop app + CLI + TypeScript SDK + MCP server. Control workspaces from anywhere. - **Broadest agent support** — Amp, Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Droid, Gemini, Copilot, Mastra, Mistral Vibe, OpenCode, Pi, Polygraph + custom agents - **Automations** — Schedule agent sessions to run on a timer - **Slack & Linear integration** — Spin up workspaces from Slack messages or Linear issues - **In-app browser + port detection** — Preview dev servers per workspace - Custom agent support with icons, workspace presets - Issue/star ratio 2.6% — good stability **❌ Weaknesses** - **macOS only** — No Windows/Linux builds. The most critical weakness. - Requires Bun runtime, Caddy, and other external dependencies - "Other" license — open-source freedom unclear - iOS app still "coming soon" > **Best for:** macOS developers who want maximum programmability (CLI + SDK + MCP) and the broadest agent ecosystem. --- ### 4. cmux — manaflow-ai/cmux | | | |---|---| | **GitHub** | [manaflow-ai/cmux](https://github.com/manaflow-ai/cmux) | | **Stars / Forks / Issues** | 24,412 / 1,970 / 3,446 (14.1%) | | **Language** | Swift (native macOS) | | **License** | GPL | | **Platform** | macOS | | **Website** | [cmux.com](https://cmux.com/) | **✅ Strengths** - **Most GitHub stars (24.4K)** — largest community - **Native performance** — Swift/AppKit + libghostty GPU rendering. Blows Electron away on startup speed and memory. - **Clear philosophy** — "A primitive, not a solution." Doesn't force a workflow on you. - **Notification system** — OSC 9/99/777 sequence detection, blue ring + tab highlight, `cmux notify` CLI - **In-app browser** — agent-browser API ported; accessibility tree snapshots, click, fill, JS eval - **SSH** — `cmux ssh user@remote` creates remote workspaces; browser routes through remote network - **Claude Code Teams** — `cmux claude-teams` one-command teammate mode, no tmux needed - Ghostty config compatible — existing themes/fonts/colors just work - Full CLI + socket API for automation **❌ Weaknesses** - **macOS only** — lowest platform score - **It's a terminal, not an IDE** — no built-in worktree management, diff viewer, or PR creation - **3,446 open issues (14.1%)** — highest ratio; complex native app management burden - No mobile companion > **Best for:** macOS power users who want native performance and a terminal-first, non-prescriptive approach. Pair with Orca/Emdash/Superset for diff review. --- ### 5. Parallel Code — johannesjo/parallel-code | | | |---|---| | **GitHub** | [johannesjo/parallel-code](https://github.com/johannesjo/parallel-code) | | **Stars / Forks / Issues** | 838 / 111 / 39 (4.7%) | | **Language** | TypeScript (Electron + SolidJS) | | **License** | MIT | | **Platform** | macOS · Linux | | **Website** | [parallelcode.app](https://parallelcode.app/) | **✅ Strengths** - **Docker sandboxing** — Project-specific `.parallel-code/Dockerfile` for full isolation. No other OSS tool has this. - **AI Arena** — Race agents head-to-head, merge the winner - **Coverage radar** — Per-file test-coverage badges in the Changed Files panel - **Steps tracking panel** — Engineering-manager-style timeline of agent progress - Mobile monitoring via QR code (Wi-Fi/Tailscale) - 10 themes, keyboard-first, focus mode - MIT license, completely free **❌ Weaknesses** - Smallest community (838★) — long-term maintenance uncertain - No Windows support - No SSH/remote worktrees - Single-developer project (johannesjo) > **Best for:** Developers experimenting with Docker-isolated agents and head-to-head agent competition. --- ### 6. Claude Squad — smtg-ai/claude-squad | | | |---|---| | **GitHub** | [smtg-ai/claude-squad](https://github.com/smtg-ai/claude-squad) | | **Stars / Forks / Issues** | 8,108 / 585 / 52 (0.6%) | | **Language** | Go (TUI) | | **License** | AGPL-3.0 | | **Platform** | macOS · Linux (tmux required) | | **Website** | [smtg-ai.github.io/claude-squad](https://smtg-ai.github.io/claude-squad/) | **✅ Strengths** - **Best stability** — 0.6% issue/star ratio, Go binary, simple architecture (tmux + worktree) - **Fastest setup** — `brew install claude-squad` → 6 sessions in 5 seconds - Keyboard-first TUI with vim-style navigation - Supports Claude Code, Codex, Aider, Gemini, OpenCode, Amp - `--autoyes` flag for unattended auto-accept (experimental) **❌ Weaknesses** - **Weak visual diff review** — need a separate tool for the review step - **No remote/SSH/mobile** — local single machine only - AGPL-3.0 — legal review needed for commercial embedding - No Windows support (tmux dependency) > **Best for:** Terminal power users who want the fastest, most stable path to parallel agents. Solo developers on macOS/Linux. --- ### 7. Vibe Kanban — BloopAI/vibe-kanban | | | |---|---| | **GitHub** | [BloopAI/vibe-kanban](https://github.com/BloopAI/vibe-kanban) | | **Language** | TypeScript (Web app) | | **License** | Apache-2.0 | | **Platform** | Web (cross-platform) | | **Status** | Community-maintained (Bloop shut down April 2026) | **✅ Strengths** - Kanban card = worktree = agent — intuitive metaphor - MCP server for autonomous card decomposition (closest to autonomous task decomposition) - 10+ agents supported, in-app browser with device emulation - Self-hostable, team-friendly **❌ Weaknesses** - **Bloop shut down** — paid subscriptions refunded, cloud features wound down - No hard task dependencies (task B waiting for task A) - 20 worktrees → disk bloat - Future maintenance velocity entirely depends on community contributors > **Best for:** Teams wanting a shared visual board with MCP-driven task decomposition, comfortable with community-maintained software. --- ### 8. Nimbalyst — nimbalyst.com | | | |---|---| | **Website** | [nimbalyst.com](https://nimbalyst.com/) | | **Platform** | Cross-platform desktop + iOS | | **Predecessor** | Crystal (deprecated Feb 2026) | **✅ Strengths** - Worktree as a first-class session primitive — one-click option when starting a session - Inline diffs, visual files sidebar, GUI review-and-merge - Cross-platform + iOS companion - Built-in markdown/Excalidraw/mockup visual editing **❌ Weaknesses** - Youngest project (rebranded from Crystal in Feb 2026) - Limited community, documentation, and reputation data - Vendor-led, license unclear - Narrow agent support (Claude Code, Codex) > **Best for:** Developers who want worktree-first session management with visual editing. Re-evaluate in 6 months as the project matures. --- ### 9. Intent — Augment Code | | | |---|---| | **Website** | [intentapp.dev](https://www.intentapp.dev/) | | **Platform** | macOS (Windows waitlist, Linux not announced) | | **Status** | Public beta | | **Pricing** | Augment credits ($0 trial / $20–$60/mo) | **✅ Strengths** - **Spec-driven coordination** — A living spec is the single source of truth for every agent. Edit the spec once; updates propagate to all active agents. - **Coordinator → Implementor → Verifier** three-agent division of labor - Shines in cross-service refactors — field renames propagate via spec - BYOA (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode + Auggie) - Context Engine exposed as MCP server **❌ Weaknesses** - **Closed source** — no code audit, vendor lock-in - **macOS only** — Windows waitlist, Linux not announced - Uses Augment credits — cost overlap with existing Claude/Cursor subscriptions - Public beta — production stability unverified > **Best for:** Teams doing complex cross-service refactors who benefit from spec-driven coordination and are on macOS. --- ### 10. Conductor — Melty Labs | | | |---|---| | **Website** | [conductor.build](https://www.conductor.build/) | | **Platform** | macOS | | **License** | Closed source | | **Pricing** | Free (BYO login) | **✅ Strengths** - Most polished Mac dashboard — diff-first review, clean UX throughout - Free with BYO Claude/Codex/Cursor/OpenCode login - Best orchestrator-only tool to sit beside your existing VS Code or JetBrains setup **❌ Weaknesses** - **Closed source** — no code audit, vendor lock-in - **macOS only** — cross-platform "trickling in" - No CLI/SDK/MCP — not programmable - No remote/SSH/mobile - Narrow agent support (4 agents) > **Best for:** macOS developers wanting the easiest, most polished entry point to parallel agent orchestration beside their existing editor. --- ## 🎯 Decision Guide | Your Situation | Recommended Tool | Why | |----------------|-------------------|-----| | Cross-platform team, need mobile monitoring | **Orca** | Only tool with 3 OS + iOS/Android | | Enterprise, multiple ticket systems | **Emdash** | 8 ticket platforms + port injection + privacy | | macOS + programmable automation | **Superset** | CLI + SDK + MCP, Automations | | macOS + native performance + terminal-first | **cmux** | Swift/AppKit, GPU rendering, notifications | | Terminal-only, fastest possible start | **Claude Squad** | 5-second setup, best stability | | Docker isolation + agent competition | **Parallel Code** | AI Arena, project-specific Dockerfiles | | Spec-driven cross-service refactors | **Intent** | Living spec propagation, Coordinator/Verifier | | Free, easiest Mac onboarding | **Conductor** | Free + BYO login, best polish | --- ## 📈 Feature Comparison Matrix | Tool | Worktree | Diff Review | Remote/SSH | Mobile | Browser | CLI/SDK/MCP | Automations | Ticket Integration | |------|:--------:|:-----------:|:----------:|:------:|:-------:|:-----------:|:-----------:|:-----------------:| | **Orca** | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ iOS+Android | ✅ Design Mode | ✅ CLI | ❌ | GitHub·Linear | | **Emdash** | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ SSH/SFTP | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | 8 platforms | | **Superset** | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | 🔄 iOS soon | ✅ | ✅ CLI+SDK+MCP | ✅ | Slack·Linear | | **cmux** | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ CLI+Socket | ❌ | ❌ | | **Parallel Code** | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ QR | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | | **Claude Squad** | ✅ | ⚠️ weak | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | | **Vibe Kanban** | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ MCP | ❌ | ❌ | | **Nimbalyst** | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ iOS | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | | **Intent** | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ MCP | ❌ | ❌ | | **Conductor** | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | --- ## 🌐 Broader Ecosystem These are **not** agent-native IDEs, but they've absorbed worktree/parallel-agent features and are worth knowing: | Tool | Category | Agent-Native Feature | |------|----------|---------------------| | [Cursor 3](https://cursor.com/) | Dedicated IDE | Agents Window with `/worktree` and `/best-of-n` commands (Apr 2026) | | [Windsurf Wave 13](https://windsurf.com/) | Dedicated IDE | Parallel Cascade sessions with worktrees, dedicated agent terminal | | [Google Antigravity](https://antigravity.google/) | Dedicated IDE | Multi-agent parallel workspaces, free, dynamic subagents | | [Cline](https://cline.bot/) | VS Code Extension | Model-agnostic, open-source agent in VS Code | | [Roo Code](https://roocode.com/) | VS Code Extension | Multi-persona agent system (Coder, Architect, Debugger) | | [Claude Code](https://claude.com/product/claude-code) | Terminal Agent | Built-in `isolation: worktree` in subagent frontmatter | | [Aider](https://aider.chat/) | Terminal Agent | Editor-agnostic, Git-native CLI pair programming | --- ## 📅 Market Observations (July 2026) 1. **Orca and Emdash form the top tier** — both cross-platform, open-source, rich features, good stability. 2. **cmux dominates community size and performance** but is macOS-only and terminal-only (not a full IDE). 3. **Superset has the deepest feature set** but macOS-only is a critical limitation. 4. **Closed-source tools (Conductor, Intent) score lowest** on open-source freedom — a structural disadvantage. 5. **Git worktrees are the consensus isolation primitive** — every serious tool now uses them. 6. **Port collision is the next frontier** — only Emdash (`$EMDASH_PORT`) solves it at the config level. 7. **Mobile monitoring is emerging** — Orca (iOS+Android), Parallel Code (QR), Nimbalyst (iOS) lead; others have nothing. 8. **The category is consolidating** — Crystal → Nimbalyst, Bloop → community Vibe Kanban. Expect more M&A and shutdowns in H2 2026. --- ## 🤝 Contributing Contributions are welcome! If you'd like to add a tool, update scores, or fix an inaccuracy: 1. Open an [issue](https://github.com/pinion05/awesome-agent-ide/issues) with your suggestion 2. Or submit a PR with updated data and sources Please include **verifiable sources** (GitHub repos, documentation, review articles) for any score changes. --- ## 📄 License [CC0-1.0](LICENSE) — Public domain. Use it however you want. ---
**[🌐 View as Website](https://pinion05.github.io/awesome-agent-ide/)** · **[⭐ Star on GitHub](https://github.com/pinion05/awesome-agent-ide)** *Last updated: July 2026 · Maintained by [pinion05](https://github.com/pinion05)*

arguments.content:
🚀 Awesome Agent-Native IDE
  • 개요
  • 순위
  • 평가
  • 상세 분석
  • 기능 비교
  • 추천 가이드
  • 시장 관찰

🚀 Awesome Agent-Native IDE

AI 코딩 에이전트 시대를 위해 태어난 IDE & 오케스트레이터 종합 비교

📊 10개 도구 분석 🗓️ 2026년 7월 기준 ⭐ GitHub에서 보기

📖 Agent-Native IDE란?

전통적 IDE(Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code)가 AI 기능을 기존 에디터에 "붙이는" 접근이라면, Agent-Native IDE는 여러 CLI 코딩 에이전트(Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI 등)를 병렬로 실행·관리·리뷰하기 위해 처음부터 설계된 환경입니다.

🌳

Git Worktree 격리

각 에이전트마다 독립된 브랜치와 작업 디렉토리. 하나의 .git 데이터베이스를 공유하며 충돌 없이 병렬 실행.

⚡

멀티 에이전트 동시 실행

한 화면에서 5~20개 이상의 에이전트를 모니터링. 각각 다른 태스크를 동시에 수행.

🔍

Diff 리뷰 + 머지

에이전트가 만든 변경사항을 한 곳에서 검토하고 머지. 인라인 코멘트로 피드백 전송.

🔌

BYOA (Bring Your Own Agent)

자기 구독으로 어떤 CLI 에이전트든 실행. 벤더 종속 없음.

장점

  • Design Mode — Chromium에서 UI 요소 클릭 시 HTML/CSS/스크린샷을 에이전트 프롬프트로 직송
  • SSH Worktrees — 원격 머신에서 에이전트 실행, 자동 재연결 + 포트 포워딩
  • Computer Use — 에이전트가 데스크톱 앱과 실제 UI 조작
  • GitHub & Linear 네이티브 — PR/이슈/보드 앱 내 브라우징
  • Annotate AI Diffs — diff 라인에 코멘트 → 에이전트에게 피드백
  • 일일 릴리스 — 매일 기능 출시

  • MIT이지만 stablyai 단일 주도 — 벤더 의존도

🎯 사용 사례별 추천

크로스플랫폼 팀, 모바일 모니터링 필요
→ Orca
유일하게 3 OS + iOS/Android 지원
엔터프라이즈, 다수 티켓 시스템 통합
→ Emdash
8개 티켓 플랫폼 + 포트 주입 + 프라이버시
macOS + 프로그래밍 가능한 자동화
→ Superset
CLI + SDK + MCP 3면 제공, Automations
macOS + 네이티브 성능 + 터미널 퍼스트
→ cmux
Swift/AppKit, GPU 렌더링, 알림 시스템
터미널만으로 빠르게 시작
→ Claude Squad
5초 설치, 가장 안정적 (0.6% 이슈율)
Docker 격리 + 에이전트 경쟁 실험
→ Parallel Code
AI Arena, 프로젝트별 Dockerfile
스펙 기반 크로스 서비스 리팩토링
→ Intent
Living spec 전파, Coordinator/Verifier
무료, 가장 쉬운 Mac 시작
→ Conductor
무료 + BYO 로그인, 최고 폴리싱

📅 2026년 7월 시장 관찰

  1. Orca와 Emdash가 1티어 형성 — 크로스플랫폼 + 오픈소스 + 풍부한 기능 + 양호한 안정성.
  2. cmux는 커뮤니티 규모와 성능으로 독보적 — 하지만 macOS 전용 + 터미널 한계로 "IDE" 카테고리에서는 4위.
  3. Superset은 기능 깊이 최상급 — 하지만 macOS 전용이 치명적. Windows/Linux 지원이 시급.
  4. 폐쇄 소스 도구(Conductor, Intent)가 최하위 — 오픈소스 자유도에서 근본적 불리.
  5. Git worktree가 합의된 격리 원시 도구 — 모든 serious tool이 채택.
  6. 포트 충돌이 다음 프론티어 — Emdash($EMDASH_PORT)만이 설정 레벨에서 해결.
  7. 모바일 모니터링이 부상 — Orca(iOS+Android), Parallel Code(QR), Nimbalyst(iOS)가 선도.
  8. 카테고리 통합 진행 중 — Crystal→Nimbalyst, Bloop→커뮤니티 Vibe Kanban. 2026 H2 추가 M&A와 종료 예상.

URL: https://pinion05.github.io/awesome-agent-ide/
Content-Type: text/html
Method: jina

---

Title: 🚀 Awesome Agent-Native IDE — 2026 Ranked Comparison

URL Source: https://pinion05.github.io/awesome-agent-ide/

Markdown Content:
🚀 Awesome Agent-Native IDE

*   [개요](https://pinion05.github.io/awesome-agent-ide/#what)
*   [순위](https://pinion05.github.io/awesome-agent-ide/#ranking)
*   [평가](https://pinion05.github.io/awesome-agent-ide/#scores)
*   [상세 분석](https://pinion05.github.io/awesome-agent-ide/#tools)
*   [기능 비교](https://pinion05.github.io/awesome-agent-ide/#matrix)
*   [추천 가이드](https://pinion05.github.io/awesome-agent-ide/#guide)
*   [시장 관찰](https://pinion05.github.io/awesome-agent-ide/#observations)

AI 코딩 에이전트 시대를 위해 태어난 IDE & 오케스트레이터 종합 비교

📊 10개 도구 분석🗓️ 2026년 7월 기준⭐ GitHub에서 보기

## 📖 Agent-Native IDE란?

전통적 IDE(Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code)가 AI 기능을 기존 에디터에 "붙이는" 접근이라면, **Agent-Native IDE**는 여러 CLI 코딩 에이전트(Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI 등)를 **병렬로 실행·관리·리뷰하기 위해 처음부터 설계된** 환경입니다.

🌳

#### Git Worktree 격리

각 에이전트마다 독립된 브랜치와 작업 디렉토리. 하나의 .git 데이터베이스를 공유하며 충돌 없이 병렬 실행.

⚡

#### 멀티 에이전트 동시 실행

한 화면에서 5~20개 이상의 에이전트를 모니터링. 각각 다른 태스크를 동시에 수행.

🔍

#### Diff 리뷰 + 머지

에이전트가 만든 변경사항을 한 곳에서 검토하고 머지. 인라인 코멘트로 피드백 전송.

🔌

#### BYOA (Bring Your Own Agent)

자기 구독으로 어떤 CLI 에이전트든 실행. 벤더 종속 없음.

## 🏆 종합 순위

| 순위 | 도구 | 점수 | 플랫폼 | 라이선스 | Stars | 한줄평 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 🥇 | [**Orca**](https://github.com/stablyai/orca) | 8.6 | 🍎 🪟 🐧 📱 | MIT | 18.3K | 풀 크로스플랫폼 + 모바일, Design Mode, 일일 릴리스 |
| 🥈 | [**Emdash**](https://github.com/generalaction/emdash) | 8.3 | 🍎 🪟 🐧 | Apache-2.0 | 5.1K | 포트 주입, 8개 티켓 통합, 28+ 에이전트 |
| 🥉 | [**Superset**](https://github.com/superset-sh/superset) | 7.5 | 🍎 | Other | 12.4K | CLI + SDK + MCP, 자동화, 최다 에이전트 지원 |
| 4 | [**cmux**](https://github.com/manaflow-ai/cmux) | 7.0 | 🍎 | GPL | 24.4K | 네이티브 Swift 성능, 최대 커뮤니티, 터미널 퍼스트 |
| 5 | [**Parallel Code**](https://github.com/johannesjo/parallel-code) | 6.7 | 🍎 🐧 | MIT | 0.8K | Docker 샌드박싱, AI Arena, 커버리지 레이더 |
| 6 | [**Claude Squad**](https://github.com/smtg-ai/claude-squad) | 6.5 | 🍎 🐧 | AGPL-3.0 | 8.1K | 최고 안정성 (0.6%), 최단 설정 |
| 7 | [**Vibe Kanban**](https://github.com/BloopAI/vibe-kanban) | 6.3 | 🌐 | Apache-2.0 | — | 칸반 + MCP 자동 분해 (커뮤니티 유지보수) |
| 8 | [**Nimbalyst**](https://nimbalyst.com/) | 6.2 | 🍎 🪟 🐧 📱 | — | — | Worktree 일급 시민, 시각 편집, iOS 동반 앱 |
| 9 | [**Intent**](https://www.intentapp.dev/) | 6.1 | 🍎 | Closed | — | 스펙 기반 조정, Coordinator/Verifier 에이전트 |
| 10 | [**Conductor**](https://www.conductor.build/) | 5.7 | 🍎 | Closed | — | Mac 최고 폴리싱, 무료, BYO 로그인 |

## 📊 다차원 평가

각 도구를 7개 지표로 평가 (10점 만점), 가중치를 적용해 종합 점수 산출.

| 도구 | 기술력 (20%) | 안정성 (15%) | 편의성 (15%) | 생태계 (15%) | 확장성 (15%) | 플랫폼 (10%) | 오픈소스 (10%) | 종합 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

#### 장점

*   유일한 풀 크로스플랫폼 + 모바일 (iOS/Android 동반 앱)
*   Design Mode — Chromium에서 UI 요소 클릭 시 HTML/CSS/스크린샷을 에이전트 프롬프트로 직송
*   SSH Worktrees — 원격 머신에서 에이전트 실행, 자동 재연결 + 포트 포워딩
*   Computer Use — 에이전트가 데스크톱 앱과 실제 UI 조작
*   GitHub & Linear 네이티브 — PR/이슈/보드 앱 내 브라우징
*   Annotate AI Diffs — diff 라인에 코멘트 → 에이전트에게 피드백
*   일일 릴리스 — 매일 기능 출시

#### 단점

*   Electron 기반 — 네이티브 대비 메모리/시작 속도 열세
*   1,361개 미해결 이슈 — 관리 부담
*   MIT이지만 stablyai 단일 주도 — 벤더 의존도

**추천:** 분산 팀, 모바일 모니터링 필요, 크로스플랫폼 + 최강 기능 세트가 필요한 경우

#### 장점

*   가장 넓은 티켓 통합 — Linear, GitHub, Jira, GitLab, Asana, Monday.com, Forgejo, Plain (8개)
*   $EMDASH_PORT 주입 — 포트 충돌 문제를 설정 레벨에서 해결
*   28+ CLI 에이전트 자동 감지 (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Devin 등)
*   SSH/SFTP 원격 프로젝트 — 원격 코드베이스에서 동일한 병렬 워크플로우
*   Local-first + 프라이버시 — SQLite 로컬 저장, 서버 전송 없음
*   크로스플랫폼 + Apache-2.0 — 가장 관대한 조합

#### 단점

*   모바일 동반 앱 없음
*   에이전트 간 조정(협업) 불가 — 각자 독립 실행
*   2026년 초 출시 — 커뮤니티 성장 단계

**추천:** 엔터프라이즈 팀, 다수 티켓 시스템 통합, 포트 격리, 프라이버시 보장이 필요한 경우

#### 장점

*   가장 다양한 접근면 — 데스크톱 + CLI + TypeScript SDK + MCP 서버
*   가장 넓은 에이전트 지원 — 12+ 사전 설정 + 커스텀 에이전트
*   Automations — 스케줄 기반 에이전트 세션 실행
*   Slack & Linear 통합 — 메시지/이슈에서 워크스페이스 생성
*   인앱 브라우저 + 포트 감지 — 워크스페이스별 dev 서버 미리보기

#### 단점

*   macOS 전용 — Windows/Linux 빌드 없음 (치명적)
*   Bun, Caddy 등 외부 의존성 필요
*   "Other" 라이선스 — 오픈소스 자유도 불투명
*   iOS 앱 아직 "출시 예정"

**추천:** macOS 개발자, 최대 프로그래밍 가능성(CLI+SDK+MCP)과 최다 에이전트 생태계가 필요한 경우

# 🚀 Awesome Agent-Native IDE ### A curated, ranked comparison of IDEs and orchestrators built for the AI coding agent era. [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/pinion05/awesome-agent-ide?style=for-the-badge&logo=github)](https://github.com/pinion05/awesome-agent-ide) [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/CC0-1.0-blue?style=for-the-badge)](LICENSE) [![Last Updated](https://img.shields.io/badge/Updated-2026--07-lightgrey?style=for-the-badge)](#) [![Website](https://img.shields.io/badge/🌐%20Website-GitHub%20Pages-success?style=for-the-badge)](https://pinion05.github.io/awesome-agent-ide/)
--- ## 📖 What is an Agent-Native IDE? Traditional IDEs (Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code) bolt AI features onto an existing editor. **Agent-Native IDEs** are built from the ground up to run, manage, and review **multiple CLI coding agents** (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) in parallel. The defining characteristics: | Feature | Description | |---------|-------------| | **Git Worktree Isolation** | Each agent gets its own branch and working directory, sharing one `.git` database | | **Multi-Agent Parallel Execution** | Run 5–20+ agents simultaneously, monitored from one screen | | **Diff Review + Merge Workflow** | Review agent-generated changes in one place before merging | | **BYOA (Bring Your Own Agent)** | Run any CLI agent with your own subscription — no vendor lock-in | > **Not covered here:** Traditional IDEs with AI bolted on (Cursor, Windsurf, Copilot). Those are excellent tools, but this list focuses on tools whose *primary purpose* is agent orchestration. --- ## 🏆 Ranking Overview | Rank | Tool | Score | Platform | License | Stars | One-Liner | |:----:|------|:-----:|----------|---------|:-----:|-----------| | 🥇 | [**Orca**](https://github.com/stablyai/orca) | **8.6** | macOS·Win·Linux·📱 | MIT | 18.3K | Full cross-platform + mobile, Design Mode, daily releases | | 🥈 | [**Emdash**](https://github.com/generalaction/emdash) | **8.3** | macOS·Win·Linux | Apache-2.0 | 5.1K | Port injection, 8 ticket integrations, 28+ agents | | 🥉 | [**Superset**](https://github.com/superset-sh/superset) | **7.5** | macOS | Other | 12.4K | CLI + SDK + MCP, automations, broadest agent support | | 4 | [**cmux**](https://github.com/manaflow-ai/cmux) | **7.0** | macOS | GPL | 24.4K | Native Swift performance, largest community, terminal-first | | 5 | [**Parallel Code**](https://github.com/johannesjo/parallel-code) | **6.7** | macOS·Linux | MIT | 0.8K | Docker sandboxing, AI Arena, coverage radar | | 6 | [**Claude Squad**](https://github.com/smtg-ai/claude-squad) | **6.5** | macOS·Linux | AGPL-3.0 | 8.1K | Best stability (0.6% issue ratio), fastest setup | | 7 | [**Vibe Kanban**](https://github.com/BloopAI/vibe-kanban) | **6.3** | Web | Apache-2.0 | — | Kanban + MCP auto-decomposition (community-maintained) | | 8 | [**Nimbalyst**](https://nimbalyst.com/) | **6.2** | Cross-platform·📱 | — | — | Worktree-first sessions, visual editing, iOS companion | | 9 | [**Intent**](https://www.intentapp.dev/) | **6.1** | macOS | Closed | — | Spec-driven coordination, Coordinator/Verifier agents | | 10 | [**Conductor**](https://www.conductor.build/) | **5.7** | macOS | Closed | — | Best Mac polish, free, BYO login | --- ## 📊 Scoring Methodology Each tool is scored on **7 dimensions** (1–10 scale), weighted to produce a composite score. | Dimension | Weight | What We Evaluate | |-----------|:------:|------------------| | **Technical Depth** | 20% | Isolation model (worktree/container), coordination depth, architecture quality, unique tech (Design Mode, Computer Use, port injection) | | **Stability** | 15% | Issue-to-star ratio, release cadence, codebase maturity, runtime stability (native vs Electron) | | **UX / Convenience** | 15% | Setup ease, visual diff review, keyboard shortcuts, notification system, workflow polish | | **Ecosystem** | 15% | Supported agents, ticket integrations (Linear/GitHub/Jira), CLI/SDK/MCP availability, community size | | **Scalability** | 15% | Remote execution (SSH), mobile companion, parallel capacity, programmability | | **Platform Support** | 10% | OS coverage (macOS / Windows / Linux / mobile) | | **Open Source Health** | 10% | License freedom, vendor independence, community-driven development | ### Detailed Scores | Tool | Technical (20%) | Stability (15%) | UX (15%) | Ecosystem (15%) | Scalability (15%) | Platform (10%) | OSS (10%) | **Total** | |------|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:| | **Orca** | 9 | 7 | 9 | 8 | 9 | 10 | 8 | **8.6** | | **Emdash** | 8 | 7 | 8 | 9 | 8 | 10 | 9 | **8.3** | | **Superset** | 8 | 8 | 8 | 9 | 8 | 3 | 6 | **7.5** | | **cmux** | 8 | 6 | 8 | 8 | 7 | 3 | 7 | **7.0** | | **Parallel Code** | 7 | 7 | 7 | 6 | 6 | 6 | 8 | **6.7** | | **Claude Squad** | 6 | 9 | 6 | 7 | 5 | 6 | 6 | **6.5** | | **Vibe Kanban** | 7 | 4 | 7 | 7 | 5 | 8 | 6 | **6.3** | | **Nimbalyst** | 7 | 5 | 7 | 5 | 6 | 8 | 5 | **6.2** | | **Intent** | 9 | 5 | 7 | 6 | 7 | 3 | 2 | **6.1** | | **Conductor** | 7 | 7 | 8 | 5 | 5 | 3 | 2 | **5.7** | --- ## 🔍 Detailed Analysis ### 🥇 1. Orca — stablyai/orca | | | |---|---| | **GitHub** | [stablyai/orca](https://github.com/stablyai/orca) | | **Stars / Forks / Issues** | 18,327 / 1,448 / 1,361 (7.4%) | | **Language** | TypeScript (Electron) | | **License** | MIT | | **Platform** | macOS · Windows · Linux · iOS · Android | | **Website** | [onorca.dev](https://www.onorca.dev/) | **✅ Strengths** - **Only full cross-platform + mobile** — iOS/Android companion app for monitoring and follow-ups from anywhere - **Design Mode** — Click any UI element in a real Chromium window; HTML/CSS/screenshot sent straight to the agent's prompt. Unique to Orca. - **SSH Worktrees** — Run agents on a beefy remote box with auto-reconnect and port forwarding - **Computer Use** — Agents can operate desktop apps and visible UI - **GitHub & Linear native** — Browse PRs, issues, and boards in-app; open a worktree from any task - **Annotate AI Diffs** — Drop comments on any diff line, ship them back to the agent - **Daily releases** — Ships every day; the changelog is the real feature list - Ghostty-class terminals (WebGL), infinite splits, scrollback that survives restarts **❌ Weaknesses** - Electron-based — heavier than native alternatives (cmux) - 1,361 open issues — active but management burden is real - MIT but stablyai-led — single vendor dependency > **Best for:** Distributed teams needing mobile monitoring, cross-platform support, and the richest agent-native feature set. --- ### 🥈 2. Emdash — generalaction/emdash | | | |---|---| | **GitHub** | [generalaction/emdash](https://github.com/generalaction/emdash) | | **Stars / Forks / Issues** | 5,149 / 534 / 132 (2.6%) | | **Language** | TypeScript (Electron) | | **License** | Apache-2.0 | | **Platform** | macOS · Windows · Linux | | **Website** | [emdash.sh](https://emdash.sh/) | | **Backing** | YC W26 | **✅ Strengths** - **Broadest ticket integration** — Linear, GitHub, Jira, GitLab, Asana, Monday.com, Forgejo, Plain (8 platforms) - **`$EMDASH_PORT` injection** — Solves the port-collision problem at the config level, which almost no other tool handles - **Broadest agent support** — 28+ CLI providers auto-detected (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, Amp, Devin, Qwen Code, Droid, Copilot, etc.) - **SSH/SFTP remote projects** — Same parallel workflow on remote codebases, OS keychain auth - **Local-first + privacy** — SQLite local storage, no code/chat sent to Emdash servers - Cross-platform + Apache-2.0 — most permissive license combination - Issue/star ratio 2.6% — excellent stability indicator **❌ Weaknesses** - No mobile companion app - No agent-to-agent coordination — each runs independently - Launched early 2026; community still growing > **Best for:** Enterprise teams needing ticket integration, port isolation, and cross-platform support with strong privacy guarantees. --- ### 🥉 3. Superset — superset-sh/superset | | | |---|---| | **GitHub** | [superset-sh/superset](https://github.com/superset-sh/superset) | | **Stars / Forks / Issues** | 12,399 / 1,076 / 323 (2.6%) | | **Language** | TypeScript (Electron) | | **License** | Other | | **Platform** | macOS | | **Website** | [superset.sh](https://superset.sh/) | | **Backing** | YC | **✅ Strengths** - **Most access surfaces** — Desktop app + CLI + TypeScript SDK + MCP server. Control workspaces from anywhere. - **Broadest agent support** — Amp, Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Droid, Gemini, Copilot, Mastra, Mistral Vibe, OpenCode, Pi, Polygraph + custom agents - **Automations** — Schedule agent sessions to run on a timer - **Slack & Linear integration** — Spin up workspaces from Slack messages or Linear issues - **In-app browser + port detection** — Preview dev servers per workspace - Custom agent support with icons, workspace presets - Issue/star ratio 2.6% — good stability **❌ Weaknesses** - **macOS only** — No Windows/Linux builds. The most critical weakness. - Requires Bun runtime, Caddy, and other external dependencies - "Other" license — open-source freedom unclear - iOS app still "coming soon" > **Best for:** macOS developers who want maximum programmability (CLI + SDK + MCP) and the broadest agent ecosystem. --- ### 4. cmux — manaflow-ai/cmux | | | |---|---| | **GitHub** | [manaflow-ai/cmux](https://github.com/manaflow-ai/cmux) | | **Stars / Forks / Issues** | 24,412 / 1,970 / 3,446 (14.1%) | | **Language** | Swift (native macOS) | | **License** | GPL | | **Platform** | macOS | | **Website** | [cmux.com](https://cmux.com/) | **✅ Strengths** - **Most GitHub stars (24.4K)** — largest community - **Native performance** — Swift/AppKit + libghostty GPU rendering. Blows Electron away on startup speed and memory. - **Clear philosophy** — "A primitive, not a solution." Doesn't force a workflow on you. - **Notification system** — OSC 9/99/777 sequence detection, blue ring + tab highlight, `cmux notify` CLI - **In-app browser** — agent-browser API ported; accessibility tree snapshots, click, fill, JS eval - **SSH** — `cmux ssh user@remote` creates remote workspaces; browser routes through remote network - **Claude Code Teams** — `cmux claude-teams` one-command teammate mode, no tmux needed - Ghostty config compatible — existing themes/fonts/colors just work - Full CLI + socket API for automation **❌ Weaknesses** - **macOS only** — lowest platform score - **It's a terminal, not an IDE** — no built-in worktree management, diff viewer, or PR creation - **3,446 open issues (14.1%)** — highest ratio; complex native app management burden - No mobile companion > **Best for:** macOS power users who want native performance and a terminal-first, non-prescriptive approach. Pair with Orca/Emdash/Superset for diff review. --- ### 5. Parallel Code — johannesjo/parallel-code | | | |---|---| | **GitHub** | [johannesjo/parallel-code](https://github.com/johannesjo/parallel-code) | | **Stars / Forks / Issues** | 838 / 111 / 39 (4.7%) | | **Language** | TypeScript (Electron + SolidJS) | | **License** | MIT | | **Platform** | macOS · Linux | | **Website** | [parallelcode.app](https://parallelcode.app/) | **✅ Strengths** - **Docker sandboxing** — Project-specific `.parallel-code/Dockerfile` for full isolation. Claude Squad — smtg-ai/claude-squad | | | |---|---| | **GitHub** | [smtg-ai/claude-squad](https://github.com/smtg-ai/claude-squad) | | **Stars / Forks / Issues** | 8,108 / 585 / 52 (0.6%) | | **Language** | Go (TUI) | | **License** | AGPL-3.0 | | **Platform** | macOS · Linux (tmux required) | | **Website** | [smtg-ai.github.io/claude-squad](https://smtg-ai.github.io/claude-squad/) | **✅ Strengths** - **Best stability** — 0.6% issue/star ratio, Go binary, simple architecture (tmux + worktree) - **Fastest setup** — `brew install claude-squad` → 6 sessions in 5 seconds - Keyboard-first TUI with vim-style navigation - Supports Claude Code, Codex, Aider, Gemini, OpenCode, Amp - `--autoyes` flag for unattended auto-accept (experimental) **❌ Weaknesses** - **Weak visual diff review** — need a separate tool for the review step - **No remote/SSH/mobile** — local single machine only - AGPL-3.0 — legal review needed for commercial embedding - No Windows support (tmux dependency) > **Best for:** Terminal power users who want the fastest, most stable path to parallel agents. Solo developers on macOS/Linux. --- ### 7. Vibe Kanban — BloopAI/vibe-kanban | | | |---|---| | **GitHub** | [BloopAI/vibe-kanban](https://github.com/BloopAI/vibe-kanban) | | **Language** | TypeScript (Web app) | | **License** | Apache-2.0 | | **Platform** | Web (cross-platform) | | **Status** | Community-maintained (Bloop shut down April 2026) | **✅ Strengths** - Kanban card = worktree = agent — intuitive metaphor - MCP server for autonomous card decomposition (closest to autonomous task decomposition) - 10+ agents supported, in-app browser with device emulation - Self-hostable, team-friendly **❌ Weaknesses** - **Bloop shut down** — paid subscriptions refunded, cloud features wound down - No hard task dependencies (task B waiting for task A) - 20 worktrees → disk bloat - Future maintenance velocity entirely depends on community contributors > **Best for:** Teams wanting a shared visual board with MCP-driven task decomposition, comfortable with community-maintained software. --- ### 8. # 🚀 Awesome Agent-Native IDE ### A curated, ranked comparison of IDEs and orchestrators built for the AI coding agent era. [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/pinion05/awesome-agent-ide?style=for-the-badge&logo=github)](https://github.com/pinion05/awesome-agent-ide) [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/CC0-1.0-blue?style=for-the-badge)](LICENSE) [![Last Updated](https://img.shields.io/badge/Updated-2026--07-lightgrey?style=for-the-badge)](#) [![Website](https://img.shields.io/badge/🌐%20Website-GitHub%20Pages-success?style=for-the-badge)](https://pinion05.github.io/awesome-agent-ide/)
--- ## 📖 What is an Agent-Native IDE? Traditional IDEs (Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code) bolt AI features onto an existing editor. **Agent-Native IDEs** are built from the ground up to run, manage, and review **multiple CLI coding agents** (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) in parallel. The defining characteristics: | Feature | Description | |---------|-------------| | **Git Worktree Isolation** | Each agent gets its own branch and working directory, sharing one `.git` database | | **Multi-Agent Parallel Execution** | Run 5–20+ agents simultaneously, monitored from one screen | | **Diff Review + Merge Workflow** | Review agent-generated changes in one place before merging | | **BYOA (Bring Your Own Agent)** | Run any CLI agent with your own subscription — no vendor lock-in | > **Not covered here:** Traditional IDEs with AI bolted on (Cursor, Windsurf, Copilot). Those are excellent tools, but this list focuses on tools whose *primary purpose* is agent orchestration. --- ## 🏆 Ranking Overview | Rank | Tool | Score | Platform | License | Stars | One-Liner | |:----:|------|:-----:|----------|---------|:-----:|-----------| | 🥇 | [**Orca**](https://github.com/stablyai/orca) | **8.6** | macOS·Win·Linux·📱 | MIT | 18.3K | Full cross-platform + mobile, Design Mode, daily releases | | 🥈 | [**Emdash**](https://github.com/generalaction/emdash) | **8.3** | macOS·Win·Linux | Apache-2.0 | 5.1K | Port injection, 8 ticket integrations, 28+ agents | | 🥉 | [**Superset**](https://github.com/superset-sh/superset) | **7.5** | macOS | Other | 12.4K | CLI + SDK + MCP, automations, broadest agent support | | 4 | [**cmux**](https://github.com/manaflow-ai/cmux) | **7.0** | macOS | GPL | 24.4K | Native Swift performance, largest community, terminal-first | | 5 | [**Parallel Code**](https://github.com/johannesjo/parallel-code) | **6.7** | macOS·Linux | MIT | 0.8K | Docker sandboxing, AI Arena, coverage radar | | 6 | [**Claude Squad**](https://github.com/smtg-ai/claude-squad) | **6.5** | macOS·Linux | AGPL-3.0 | 8.1K | Best stability (0.6% issue ratio), fastest setup | | 7 | [**Vibe Kanban**](https://github.com/BloopAI/vibe-kanban) | **6.3** | Web | Apache-2.0 | — | Kanban + MCP auto-decomposition (community-maintained) | | 8 | [**Nimbalyst**](https://nimbalyst.com/) | **6.2** | Cross-platform·📱 | — | — | Worktree-first sessions, visual editing, iOS companion | | 9 | [**Intent**](https://www.intentapp.dev/) | **6.1** | macOS | Closed | — | Spec-driven coordination, Coordinator/Verifier agents | | 10 | [**Conductor**](https://www.conductor.build/) | **5.7** | macOS | Closed | — | Best Mac polish, free, BYO login | --- ## 📊 Scoring Methodology Each tool is scored on **7 dimensions** (1–10 scale), weighted to produce a composite score. | Dimension | Weight | What We Evaluate | |-----------|:------:|------------------| | **Technical Depth** | 20% | Isolation model (worktree/container), coordination depth, architecture quality, unique tech (Design Mode, Computer Use, port injection) | | **Stability** | 15% | Issue-to-star ratio, release cadence, codebase maturity, runtime stability (native vs Electron) | | **UX / Convenience** | 15% | Setup ease, visual diff review, keyboard shortcuts, notification system, workflow polish | | **Ecosystem** | 15% | Supported agents, ticket integrations (Linear/GitHub/Jira), CLI/SDK/MCP availability, community size | | **Scalability** | 15% | Remote execution (SSH), mobile companion, parallel capacity, programmability | | **Platform Support** | 10% | OS coverage (macOS / Windows / Linux / mobile) | | **Open Source Health** | 10% | License freedom, vendor independence, community-driven development | ### Detailed Scores | Tool | Technical (20%) | Stability (15%) | UX (15%) | Ecosystem (15%) | Scalability (15%) | Platform (10%) | OSS (10%) | **Total** | |------|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:| | **Orca** | 9 | 7 | 9 | 8 | 9 | 10 | 8 | **8.6** | | **Emdash** | 8 | 7 | 8 | 9 | 8 | 10 | 9 | **8.3** | | **Superset** | 8 | 8 | 8 | 9 | 8 | 3 | 6 | **7.5** | | **cmux** | 8 | 6 | 8 | 8 | 7 | 3 | 7 | **7.0** | | **Parallel Code** | 7 | 7 | 7 | 6 | 6 | 6 | 8 | **6.7** | | **Claude Squad** | 6 | 9 | 6 | 7 | 5 | 6 | 6 | **6.5** | | **Vibe Kanban** | 7 | 4 | 7 | 7 | 5 | 8 | 6 | **6.3** | | **Nimbalyst** | 7 | 5 | 7 | 5 | 6 | 8 | 5 | **6.2** | | **Intent** | 9 | 5 | 7 | 6 | 7 | 3 | 2 | **6.1** | | **Conductor** | 7 | 7 | 8 | 5 | 5 | 3 | 2 | **5.7** | --- ##
